name: Kintone No-Code Database Platform API
what_it_does: No-code business application and database platform with REST API — enabling teams to build custom database applications, form-based data collection, automated workflows, dashboards, and integrations without coding, with a rich REST API for programmatic access to all apps and records.
use_cases: Reading and writing records in Kintone apps programmatically via REST API
Automating data workflows between Kintone and external systems
Building custom integrations with Kintone's process management automation
Querying cross-app data for business intelligence and reporting
Managing Kintone app configuration and user permissions programmatically
not_for: Complex relational database with advanced SQL (use PostgreSQL or MySQL)
Enterprise ERP with built-in financial modules (use SAP or NetSuite)
Modern low-code with extensive UI components (use OutSystems or Mendix for complex apps)
best_when: A Japanese or Asia-Pacific enterprise team wants to build custom business applications and workflows without coding, with a strong REST API for technical integration and solid enterprise features.
avoid_when: You need complex relational queries, financial/ERP modules, or modern UI builder capabilities beyond data-centric forms and tables.

auth.notes: API key via X-Cybozu-API-Token header. Basic auth also supported. Per-app access token or administrator token. Webhook for record add/edit/delete events. No OAuth2. JavaScript customization of Kintone UI supported. Mobile app included.

pricing.paid_tiers: Light plan $24/user/month; Standard plan $24/user/month (different features); Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Japanese company (Cybozu Inc., Tokyo). Founded 1997. Listed on Tokyo Stock Exchange. Dominant in Japan for no-code business applications. Very popular in manufacturing, retail, and services sectors in Japan and Asia Pacific. Kintone.com is the US/global subsidiary. Strong REST API for a no-code tool. 35,000+ companies globally. Popular alternative to legacy spreadsheet-based business processes.

performance.rate_limits: 10,000 API calls per day per app; custom enterprise limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for records, apps, spaces, and users. X-Cybozu-API-Token header auth. Excellent API documentation. Per-app tokens for granular access control. Query language for record filtering. Webhook for record events. JavaScript SDK and Python client library available.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Record reads fully idempotent. App reads idempotent. Record creation not idempotent — use upsert patterns or check before creating.
known_agent_gotchas: X-Cybozu-API-Token header — not Authorization, non-standard header name
Per-app tokens required for granular access — not single global API key
10,000 API calls/day per app limit — plan data sync frequency carefully
Offset pagination (not cursor) — page through large result sets carefully
Japanese UX patterns may feel different for US/EU teams — localization consideration

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR compliant. JP, US, EU data residency. X-Cybozu-API-Token auth. Per-app access tokens enable granular least-privilege. Cybozu Inc. TSX-listed public company with enterprise security standards. Business data in records can include PII — implement appropriate access controls.
